predictions.title: By Q3 2026 a published agent-skills threat sub-matrix lands as a first-class MITRE ATT&CK extension or NIST AI-RMF appendix, and at least one major skill marketplace (Anthropic's official skills repo, ClawHub, Multica, Hermes, Hugging Face skills) ships an enforced signed + provenance-attested gate before publish.
predictions.body: By Q3 2026 a published agent-skills threat sub-matrix lands as a first-class MITRE ATT&CK extension or NIST AI-RMF appendix, and at least one major skill marketplace (Anthropic's official skills repo, ClawHub, Multica, Hermes, Hugging Face skills) ships an enforced signed + provenance-attested gate before publish. By Q3 2026 a published agent-skills threat sub-matrix lands as a first-class MITRE ATT&CK extension or NIST AI-RMF appendix, and at least one major skill marketplace (Anthropic's official skills repo, ClawHub, Multica, Hermes, Hugging Face skills) ships an enforced signed + provenance-attested gate before publish. By Q3 2026 a published agent-skills threat sub-matrix lands as a first-class MITRE ATT&CK extension or NIST AI-RMF appendix, and at least one major skill marketplace (Anthropic's official skills repo, ClawHub, Multica, Hermes, Hugging Face skills) ships an enforced signed + provenance-attested gate before publish. **By Q3 2026 a published agent-skills threat sub-matrix lands as a first-class MITRE ATT&CK extension or NIST AI-RMF appendix, and at least one major skill marketplace (Anthropic's official skills repo, ClawHub, Multica, Hermes, Hugging Face skills) ships an enforced signed + provenance-attested gate before publish.** Apr 28 ATT&CK v19 + v5.4 framework already names AI-agent context poisoning, tool invocation abuse, MCP server compromise, and malicious agent deployment as techniques; Apr 28 Novita Sandbox is the first commercial mitigation product purpose-built for OpenClaw + Hermes runtimes; Apr 28 SuiteCloud Agent Skills (Oracle NetSuite, SuiteConnect SF) is the first major non-foundation-lab vendor shipping skills-as-a-product with signed-distribution as the security frame; Apr 28 arxiv 2604.24026 (SSL representation for skills) + Apr 24 arxiv 2604.23374 NeuroTaint provide the academic scaffolding. The forcing function is the Cisco RSAC 2026 36%-prompt-injection-rate finding + ClawHavoc's 1,184 malicious skills + Apr 23-29 Google +32% IPI uptick; combined this is the same governance-gap arc that produced MITRE ATT&CK Mobile in 2017 and MITRE ATLAS in 2021. The first of these mandates likely originates from CISA / NIST or directly from the marketplace operators under enterprise-customer pressure rather than from a foundation lab. In plain language: In plain language: In plain language:
predictions.summary: By Q3 2026 a published agent-skills threat sub-matrix lands as a first-class MITRE ATT&CK extension or NIST AI-RMF appendix, and at least one major skill marketplace (Anthropic's official skills repo, ClawHub, Multica, Hermes, Hugging Face skills) ships an enforced signed + provenance-attested gate before publish.

predictions.title: By H2 2026 every major local-LLM ecosystem (vLLM, SGLang, TGI, Ollama, llama.cpp, NVIDIA TensorRT-LLM, Foundry Local) exposes a unified compressed-KV-cache deployment knob
predictions.body: **By H2 2026 every major local-LLM ecosystem (vLLM, SGLang, TGI, Ollama, llama.cpp, NVIDIA TensorRT-LLM, Foundry Local) exposes a unified compressed-KV-cache deployment knob — TurboQuant + at least one alternative — and Foundry Local (Microsoft, GA Apr 9) ships native TurboQuant integration tied to Windows ML / WinML execution providers as a Windows-default optimization.** Apr 27-29 evidence: SGLang PR #21419 in-tree; SGLang #23134 fused-Triton-decode kernel rewrite hitting 93–105% bf16 throughput; vLLM v0.19.0 (Apr 2) torch 2.11 / Python 3.14 / async scheduler default + Model Runner V2; PolyKV (arxiv 2604.24971, Apr 27) shared asymmetric KV-pool for multi-agent inference; NVIDIA kvpress at Decoding-Press maturity; llama.cpp #20969 TurboQuant discussion thread active; Unsloth 2026.4.8 inference 20-30% faster. Foundry Local is the missing enterprise-Windows entry point — Microsoft ships it as a ~20MB native library that bundles local AI into any app with hardware-matched WinML execution providers, and the obvious next step for Q2 2026 is to surface a TurboQuant flag in the Foundry Catalog. The economic forcing function is the same Mag 7 capex tape: every memory-bandwidth-bound long-context workload that moves from FP16 to 3.5-bit cuts effective per-token cost ~5-6x, and Foundry Local makes that switch invisible to end-developers. In plain language: In plain language: In plain language:
predictions.summary: **By H2 2026 every major local-LLM ecosystem (vLLM, SGLang, TGI, Ollama, llama.cpp, NVIDIA TensorRT-LLM, Foundry Local) exposes a unified compressed-KV-cache deployment knob — TurboQuant + at least one alternative — and Foundry Local (Microsoft, GA Apr 9) ships native TurboQuant integration tied to Windows ML / WinML execution providers as a Windows-default optimization.** Apr 27-29 evidence: SGLang PR #21419 in-tree; SGLang #23134 fused-Triton-decode kernel rewrite hitting 93–105% bf16 throughput; vLLM v0.19.0 (Apr 2) torch 2.11 / Python 3.14 / async scheduler default + Model Runner V2; PolyKV (arxiv 2604.24971, Apr 27) shared asymmetric KV-pool for multi-agent inference; NVIDIA kvpress at Decoding-Press maturity; llama.cpp #20969 TurboQuant discussion thread active; Unsloth 2026.4.8 inference 20-30% faster.

predictions.title: The Apr 28 WSJ OpenAI-revenue-miss report + Apr 29 Mag 7 print collision triggers a 2026 SEC + analyst push for AI-revenue disclosure rewrite
predictions.body: **The Apr 28 WSJ OpenAI-revenue-miss report + Apr 29 Mag 7 print collision triggers a 2026 SEC + analyst push for AI-revenue disclosure rewrite — by Q3 2026 at least one foundation lab (OpenAI / Anthropic / xAI) ships an audited monthly-revenue or WAU disclosure cadence, and at least one Big-3 hyperscaler separates Bedrock / Azure-OpenAI / Vertex per-token margin in 10-Q footnotes from raw cloud revenue.** Apr 28 selloff (S&P -0.49%, Nasdaq -0.9%, Oracle -4%, AMD -3%, Broadcom -4%, SoftBank -10%) was the first material crack in the AI-capex-on-faith tape; Apr 29 prints were a mixed reset (MSFT/GOOGL beat hard, META -7% AH); Apr 30 AAPL prints close the Mag 7 cycle. Microsoft already cited a $37B AI run rate (+123%) explicitly — that is the disclosure precedent. With the Cerebras IPO mid-May at $22B-$25B (24% G42 / 62% MBZUAI revenue concentration), the SEC + buyside pressure for explicit per-token margin + WAU disclosure converges around Q2-Q3 2026 earnings cycles. Powell's choice to stay on Board as a check on Trump-admin Fed investigation also raises the political-risk premium on the entire AI-capex tape; demanding harder numbers is the lever public markets have. In plain language: In plain language: In plain language:
predictions.summary: **The Apr 28 WSJ OpenAI-revenue-miss report + Apr 29 Mag 7 print collision triggers a 2026 SEC + analyst push for AI-revenue disclosure rewrite — by Q3 2026 at least one foundation lab (OpenAI / Anthropic / xAI) ships an audited monthly-revenue or WAU disclosure cadence, and at least one Big-3 hyperscaler separates Bedrock / Azure-OpenAI / Vertex per-token margin in 10-Q footnotes from raw cloud revenue.** Apr 28 selloff (S&P -0.49%, Nasdaq -0.9%, Oracle -4%, AMD -3%, Broadcom -4%, SoftBank -10%) was the first material crack in the AI-capex-on-faith tape; Apr 29 prints were a mixed reset (MSFT/GOOGL beat hard, META -7% AH); Apr 30 AAPL prints close the Mag 7 cycle.

predictions.title: Powell's Apr 29 decision to remain on the Board of Governors past his Chair-term expiration (May 15) reshapes 2026 monetary-policy market psychology
predictions.body: **Powell's Apr 29 decision to remain on the Board of Governors past his Chair-term expiration (May 15) reshapes 2026 monetary-policy market psychology — by Q4 2026 the FOMC reverts to a smaller-dissent norm (≤2 dissenters) under Warsh as Chair, but Powell's continued Board presence becomes the marginal swing vote on the Fed-investigation governance question, not on rates.** Apr 29 was the highest-dissent FOMC since Oct-1992 (8-4); Miran wanted -25bp; Hammack/Kashkari/Logan opposed easing-bias inclusion. Warsh nomination advanced from Senate Banking same day. The 2026-2027 setup: Powell on Board through term-end 2028 with Warsh in the Chair seat; the dissent posture compresses on rate calls because the Chair sets the framing; Powell's marginal vote becomes a governance signal whenever the Trump-admin Fed-investigation outcome moves. Markets price a smaller forward-guidance volatility on rates but a larger institutional-risk premium on the Fed itself. The 1992-precedent comparison stops being a curiosity and starts being a structural reference point in financial-press explanations of the new Fed governance regime. In plain language: In plain language: In plain language:
predictions.summary: **Powell's Apr 29 decision to remain on the Board of Governors past his Chair-term expiration (May 15) reshapes 2026 monetary-policy market psychology — by Q4 2026 the FOMC reverts to a smaller-dissent norm (≤2 dissenters) under Warsh as Chair, but Powell's continued Board presence becomes the marginal swing vote on the Fed-investigation governance question, not on rates.** Apr 29 was the highest-dissent FOMC since Oct-1992 (8-4); Miran wanted -25bp; Hammack/Kashkari/Logan opposed easing-bias inclusion.
